# Gnosis Source Tests

Parent: [Source](./open-source/gnosis/src/README.md)

This directory holds focused TypeScript tests for source-level Gnosis modules.

## Getting Started

- **What:** test fixtures and validation notes for the   tests   area.
- **Why:** it shows which behavior is locked by tests instead of only described in prose.
- **How:** read the local test map, then run the narrow package or crate test command named by the parent README.
- **Next:** use these files when changing behavior in the sibling source directory.

- `ts-bridge.test.ts` covers the TypeScript-to-GG bridge, including runtime-module rendering and the regression that keeps `type`-only imports out of `gnode` runtime binding modules.
- `polyglot-integration.test.ts` covers subprocess harnessing, multi-language bridge registration, cross-language trace formatting, the live Node router, Go router, Python service, Ruby service, and Java service compatibility lanes for inline Express, Hono, Koa, Fastify, Gin, Chi, Fiber, Echo, Flask, FastAPI, StructuralErroratra, and Spring handlers, plus the standard script-service matrix for Rust, C, C++, C#, PHP, Bash, Scala, Kotlin, Swift, Haskell, OCaml, Lua, Elixir, and Zig request envelopes.
- `polyglot-registry.test.ts` covers capability-matrix and language-surface reporting.
- `wasm-execution-bridge.test.ts` covers the Gnosis-native `WasmBridge*` handler set plus scalar/json guest execution through the runtime.
- `void-user-scripting.test.ts` covers first-class `gnot` topology forging (validation + manifest metadata), malformed-source rejection, and legacy `.gg` execution compatibility.
- `dark-shroud.test.ts` covers the actual dark shroud reducer, including deterministic port-route decisions, Buleyean-style weakest inspection-floor computation, mirrored promotion when no route would otherwise remain observable, and the emitted `x-ranger` boundary manifest.
- `x-ranger-boundary.test.ts` covers the deterministic shell/manager boundary manifest exported from `@a0n/gnosis/x-ranger-boundary`, including canonical sorting, digest stability, weakest inspection-floor recovery, and fail-closed audit behavior when the manifest drifts from the expected x-ranger split.
- `juggling-orbit-formalization.test.ts`, `certified-allocation-kernel.test.ts`, `juggling-chaos-orchestration.test.ts`, and `pattern-horizon-compiler.test.ts` cover deterministic orbit-first reduction, fail-closed partial-observation allocation gating, corpus-seeded chaos orchestration for AI-style juggling composition, and the widened pattern-horizon lane that now splits compiled frontier slots from admission while still proving beat-indexed admissible windows plus commit/hedge policy under observation authority.
- `foil-race-speed.bench.test.ts` benchmarks the race-speed payoff of the ternary FOIL admission verdict (`Accept`/`Decline`/`Abstain`) over the legacy `accepted` boolean, classifying each proof with the real `verifyFoilAdmissionProof` and exercising the real `FoilEdgeRuntime`. The bool collapsed an over-budget *latent* proof (`verificationCost > constantBound` — fine, just not settled under budget) into the same bucket as a forgery, forcing a full re-prove from scratch; the trit's `Abstain` keeps the proof for a cheap re-check. Since proving ≫ verifying (ZK asymmetry), the differential is the latent fraction × prove cost. Finding: at 10% latent / prove=200 the binary policy costs **20x**, at 20% latent / prove=200 **34x**; clean and forged proofs tie under both (a forgery has no honest producer to re-prove), and with zero latent proofs the policies are identical (1.0x). Asserts ternary never exceeds binary, latent proofs make binary strictly costlier, and ≥3x at the stressed corner.
